folio.label: 38v
folio.l: Why grucchen heere , his Cosyn and his wyffã

note.notetxt: wyffã2198note the abbreviation. It indicates an es ending, however ther former line has only a tail after the f in lyf: double checkODR

folio.l: Of his welfare , that louen hi~ so wele
Can he hem thank , nay god wot , neuäe a dele
That bothe his soule , and eke hem self offend
And ©et they moun , here lustes nat a mend
¢ What may I concluden , of this long serie
But after woo , I rede vs alle be merye
And thanken Iubiter , of al his grace
And er we de¹ten , from this place
I rede we make , of sorwes two
O ¹fyt Ioye , lastyng euäe moo
And loketh now , where most sorwe is here Inne
There wyl I first amenden , and bygynne
¢ Suster q¶ he , this is my ful assent
With alle the lordes here , of my ¹lement
Tat Ientil Palamouö , ©oure owen knygút
That serueth ©ow , w wyl hert & mygút
And euäe hath don , syn first ©e hi~ knewe
That ©e shuln of ©oure grace , vp on hi~ rewe
And taken hi~ for husbonde , and for lord
Lene me ©oure hand , for æis is oure acord
Lat se now , of ©oure wo~manly pitee
He is a kyngis brotheres sone , parde
And thougú he were , a pore bachelere
Syn he hath serued ©ow , so many a ©ere
And had for ©ow , so gret aduäsitee
It must ben considered , leueth me
For gentil mäcy , ou©t to passen rigút
¢ Than seide he~ thus , to Palamon the knygút
I trowe there nedeth , litel sermonyng
To maken ©ow assenten , to this thyng
Come nere , & taketh ©oure lady , by the hond
Bytwixen hem , was mad a non the bond
That hight matrimouö , or mariage
By al the counseil , and the baronage
¢ And thus with al blisse , and melodye
Hath Palamon I wedded , Emelye
And god æ al this world , hath wrougút
Sende hi~ his loue , æ hath it dere a bougút
For now is Palamouö , in aÂ wele
Leuyng in blisse , in richesse and in hele
And Emelye , hi~ loueth so tenderly
And he hire serueth , so gentilly
That æäe was , no word he~ bytwene
